records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disinherited; p. pr. & vb. n. Disinheriting.]

defs: To cut off from an inheritance or from hereditary succession; to prevent, as an heir, from coming into possession of any property or right, which, by law or custom, would devolve on him in the course of descent.
quotes: Of how fair a portion Adam disinherited his whole posterity!

defs: To deprive of heritage; to dispossess.
quotes: And disinherit Chaos, that reigns here.
